beats.text: A stream of symbols fires from a transmitter across a channel toward a receiver. That channel is the hazard zone.
A bandwidth meter appears — twisted-pair copper tops out at a few hundred kilohertz. Optical fibre stretches the scale massively wider.
A label above the fibre reads: once one thousand decibels per kilometre of loss. It drops — slamming down to just zero point two. That's the engineering miracle.
A noise wave spreads flat across every frequency on a spectrum plot. That's AWGN — thermal noise, always present, mathematically guaranteed.
Multiple signal arrows bounce off building outlines and arrive at the receiver out of sync. Multipath fading — the signal arrives garbled.
All these impairments stack on screen together. Master them, and you design systems that punch through anything.
